name: lanstudio

metadata.name: lanstudio
metadata.summary: It's a Python library for VFX project-based LAN deployment

documentation:
# Example studioooooo
## Latest version of studio in this artist have the rights to change the status of any department assigned to him.

modules.name: lanstudio.remote.playlist
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: lanstudio.remote.playlist.fetch_inputs
documentation: Fetch inputs from object storage, return a list of local paths

functions.name: lanstudio.remote.playlist.main
documentation:
Generate a playlist from shots, upload generated file in the object
storage.
We assume that this script is launched in the context of a Nomad job.
Arguments are retrieved from a payload file at JSON format.
Nevertheless this script can be run directly from the command line.

modules.name: lanstudio.remote.normalize_movie
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: lanstudio.remote.normalize_movie.main
documentation:
Normalize given preview file in two videos
We assume that this script is launched in the context of a Nomad job.
Arguments are retrieved from a payload file at JSON format.
Nevertheless this script can be run directly from the command line.

functions.name: lanstudio.remote.normalize_movie._fetch_movie_file
documentation: Download the movie file.

functions.name: lanstudio.migrations.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.
other values from the config, defined by the needs of env.py,
can be acquired:
my_important_option = config.get_main_option("my_important_option")
... etc.

functions.name: lanstudio.migrations.env.run_migrations_online
documentation:
Run migrations in 'online' mode.

In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.

functions.name: lanstudio.migrations.env.render_item
documentation: Apply custom rendering for selected items.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.indexer.indexing.init
documentation: Configure Meilisearch client.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.indexer.indexing.create_index
documentation:
Create a new index and configure it properly by setting searchable_fields
and allowing to filter on project ids.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.indexer.indexing.get_index
documentation: Get index matching given name.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.indexer.indexing.clear_index
documentation: Clear all data into the index matching given name.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.indexer.indexing.index_document
documentation: Add given document to given index.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.indexer.indexing.index_documents
documentation: Add given documents to given index.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.indexer.indexing.search
documentation:
Perform a search on given index and filter result based on project IDs.
The number of results can be specified.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.indexer.indexing.remove_document
documentation: Remove document matching given id from given index.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.stores.publisher_store.init
documentation:
Initialize key value store that will be used for the event publishing.
That way the main API takes advantage of Redis pub/sub capabilities to push
events to the event stream API.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.status_automation.StatusAutomation
documentation:
Status automations are entries that allow to describe changes that
should automatically apply after a task status is changed.

For instance, a Modeling task set to done will imply to set the Rigging
task status to ready and the *ready_for* field to be set at Layout.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.day_off.DayOff
documentation: Tells that someone will have a day off this day.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.organisation.Organisation
documentation: Model to represent current organisation settings.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.preview_file.PreviewFile

class_variables.name: lanstudio.app.models.preview_file.PreviewFile.is_movie
documentation: deprecated

class_variables.name: lanstudio.app.models.preview_file.PreviewFile.url
documentation: deprecated

class_variables.name: lanstudio.app.models.preview_file.PreviewFile.uploaded_movie_url
documentation: deprecated

class_variables.name: lanstudio.app.models.preview_file.PreviewFile.uploaded_movie_name
documentation: deprecated

Describes a file which is aimed at being reviewed. It is not a publication
neither a working file.

modules.name: lanstudio.cli
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.version
documentation: Returns current installation version.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.migrate_db
documentation:
Generate migration files to describe a new revision of the database schema
(for development only).

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.downgrade_db
documentation:
Downgrade db to previous revision of the database schema
(for development only). For revision you can use an hash or a relative migration identifier.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.create_admin
documentation: Create an admin user to allow usage of the API when database is empty.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.disable_two_factor_authentication
documentation: Disable two factor authentication for given user.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.change_password
documentation: Change the password of given user.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.set_person_as_active
documentation: Set a person as active.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.sync_with_ldap_server
documentation: For each user account in your LDAP server, it creates a new user.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.sync_full
documentation:
Retrieve all data from source instance. It expects that credentials to
connect to source instance are given through SYNC_LOGIN and SYNC_PASSWORD
environment variables.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.sync_full_files
documentation:
Retrieve all files from source instance. It expects that credentials to
connect to source instance are given through SYNC_LOGIN and SYNC_PASSWORD
environment variables.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.sync_changes
documentation:
Run a daemon that import data related to any change happening on source
instance. It expects that credentials to connect to source instance are
given through SYNC_LOGIN and SYNC_PASSWORD environment variables.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.sync_file_changes
documentation:
Run a daemon that download files related to any change happening on source
instance. It expects that credentials to connect to source instance are
given through SYNC_LOGIN and SYNC_PASSWORD environment variables.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.sync_last_events
documentation:
Retrieve last events that occured on source instance and import data related
to them. It expects that credentials to connect to source instance are
given through SYNC_LOGIN and SYNC_PASSWORD environment variables.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.sync_last_files
documentation:
Retrieve last preview files and thumbnails updloaded on source instance.
It expects that credentials to connect to source instance are
given through SYNC_LOGIN and SYNC_PASSWORD environment variables.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.download_storage_files
documentation:
Download all files from a Swift object storage and store them in a local
storage.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.dump_database
documentation:
Dump database described in lanstudio environment variables and save it to
configured object storage.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.upload_files_to_cloud_storage
documentation: Upload all files related to previews to configured object storage.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.clean_tasks_data
documentation: Reset task models data (retake count, wip start date and end date)

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.remove_old_data
documentation:
Remove old events, notifications and login logs older than 90 days
(by deafult).

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.reset_search_index
documentation: Reset search index.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.init_search_index
documentation: Init search index.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.generate_preview_extra
documentation: Generate tiles, thumbnails and metadata for all previews.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.reset_movie_files_metadata
documentation: Store height and width metadata for all movie previews in the database.

functions.name: lanstudio.cli.reset_picture_files_metadata
documentation: Store height and width metadata for all picture previews in the database.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.asset_instance.AssetInstance
documentation:
An asset instance is the representation of an asset in a given shot or
layout scene. It is useful for complex scenes where an asset needs extra
treatments only related to the given shot or layout scene.
An asset can have multiple instances in a scene or in a shot (ex: a sword in
a battle field).

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.serializer.SerializerMixin
documentation: Helpers to facilitate JSON serialization of models.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.task_type.TaskType
documentation: Categorize tasks in domain areas: modeling, animation, etc.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.attachment_file.AttachmentFile
documentation: Describes a file which is attached to a comment.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.desktop_login_log.DesktopLoginLog
documentation:
Table to log all desktop session logins. The aim is to build report that
helps validating presence form.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.login_log.LoginLog
documentation:
Table to log all web session logins. The aim is to build a table that
helps finding suspicious behaviours.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.metadata_descriptor.MetadataDescriptor
documentation:
This models allow to identify which metadata are available for a given
project and a given entity type.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.news.News
documentation: A news is created each time a comment is posted.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.playlist.Playlist
documentation: Describes a playlist. The goal is to review a set of shipped materials.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.software.Software
documentation: Describes software used by working files.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.search_filter.SearchFilter
documentation:
Filters allow to store quick search on a list: asset list, shot list,
sequence list, todo-list...

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.preview_background_file.PreviewBackgroundFile
documentation: Describe a preview background file.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.search_filter_group.SearchFilterGroup
documentation: Groups are used to store search filters into sections.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.time_spent.TimeSpent
documentation: Describes the time spent by someone on a task.

documentation:

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.project_status.ProjectStatus
documentation: Describes the state of the project (mainly open or closed).

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.event.ApiEvent
documentation:
Represent notable events occuring on database (asset creation,
task assignation, etc.).

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.project.Project
documentation: Describes a production the studio works on.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.data_import_error.DataImportError
documentation: Table to allow the storage of import errors.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.comment.Comment
documentation:
Comment can occur on any object but they are mainly used on tasks.
In the case of comment tasks, they are linked to a task status and
eventually to some preview files.
The status means that comment leads to task status change. The preview file
means that the comment relates to this preview in the context of the task.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.file_status.FileStatus
documentation: Describe the state of a given file.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.working_file.WorkingFile
documentation:
Describes the file related to the work done on a given task. It is
used as source of output files published for a given entity.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.entity.Entity

class_variables.name: lanstudio.app.models.entity.Entity.code
documentation: To store sanitized version of name

class_variables.name: lanstudio.app.models.entity.Entity.nb_frames
documentation: Specific to shots

class_variables.name: lanstudio.app.models.entity.Entity.parent_id
documentation: sequence or episode

class_variables.name: lanstudio.app.models.entity.Entity.source_id
documentation: if the entity is generated from another one (like shots from scene).

Base model to represent assets, shots, sequences, episodes and scenes.
They have different meaning but they share the same behaviour toward
tasks and files.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.department.Department
documentation: Studio department like modeling, animation, etc.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.schedule_item.ScheduleItem

class_variables.name: lanstudio.app.models.schedule_item.ScheduleItem.object_id
documentation: Sequence or Episode or Asset Type

Allow to set a start date and an end date for a task type and a project.
This information allows to build a schedule for the project.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.milestone.Milestone
documentation: Allow to set a milestone date to the production schedule.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.output_type.OutputType
documentation: Type of an output files (geometry, cache, etc.)

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.task_status.TaskStatus
documentation:
Describe the state of a task. A status marked as reviewable expects a
preview file linked to relate comment.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.task.Task
documentation:
Describes a task done by a CG artist on an entity of the CG production.
The task has a state and assigned to people. It handles notion of time like
duration, start date and end date.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.__repr__
documentation: String representation based on type and name by default.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.query
documentation: Shorthand to access session query object.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.get
documentation: Shorthand to retrieve data by id.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.get_by
documentation:
Shorthand to retrieve data by using filters. It returns the first
element of the returned data.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.get_by_case_insensitive
documentation:
Shorthand to retrieve data by using filters. It returns the first
element of the returned data without checking case for any String type value.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.get_all
documentation: Shorthand to retrieve all data for a model.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.get_all_by
documentation: Shorthand to retrieve data by using filters.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.create
documentation: Shorthand to create an entry via the database session.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.create_no_commit
documentation:
Shorthand to create an entry via the database session without commiting
the request.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.delete_all_by
documentation: Shorthand to delete data by using filters.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.get_id_map
documentation:
Build a map to easily match a field value with an id. It's useful during
mass import to build foreign keys.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.create_from_import
documentation:
Create a new instance of the model based on data that comes from the lanstudio
API.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.create_from_import_list
documentation:
Create a list of instances of the model based on data that comes from
the lanstudio API.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.delete_from_import
documentation: Delete an entry and its related base on the entry id.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.save
documentation:
Shorthand to create an entry via the database session based on current
instance fields.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.delete
documentation:
Shorthand to delete an entry via the database session based on current
instance id.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.delete_no_commit
documentation:
Shorthand to delete an entry via the database session based on current
instance id. The change is not commited.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.update
documentation:
Shorthand to update an entry via the database session based on current
instance fields.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.models.base.BaseMixin.update_no_commit
documentation:
Shorthand to update an entry via the database session based on current
instance fields. It doesn't generate a commit.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.subscription.Subscription
documentation: Allow to subscribe to an entity

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.person.Person
documentation: Describe a member of the studio (and an API user).

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.build_job.BuildJob
documentation:
A build job stores information about the state of the building
of a given playlist.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.entity_type.EntityType
documentation:
Type of entities. It can describe either an asset type, or tell if target
entity is a shot, sequence, episode or layout scene.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.custom_action.CustomAction
documentation:
Custom actions are HTTP links that can be activated outside of the API.
They are mainly aimed at being used by the web frontend to allow studio
to make custom HTTP calls.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.notification.Notification
documentation: A notification is stored each time a comment is posted.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.models.output_file.OutputFile
documentation:

classes.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.comments.resources.CommentTaskResource

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.comments.resources.CommentTaskResource.post
documentation:
Create a new comment for given task.
---
tags:
- Comments
description: It requires a text, a task_status and a person as arguments.
             This way, comments keep history of status changes.
             When the comment is created, it updates the task status with given task status.
parameters:
  - in: path
    name: task_id
    required: True
    type: string
    format: UUID
    x-example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
  - in: body
    name: Comment
    description: person ID, name, comment, revision and change status of task
    schema:
        type: object
        required:
            - task_status_id
        properties:
            task_status_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25a4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            comment:
                type: string
            person_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25a4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            created_at:
                type: string
                format: date-time
                example: "2022-07-12T13:00:00"
            checklist:
                type: object
                properties:
                    item 1:
                        type: string
responses:
    201:
        description: New comment created

Creates a new comment for given task. It requires a text, a task_status
and a person as arguments. This way, comments keep history of status
changes. When the comment is created, it updates the task status with
given task status.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.comments.resources.CommentManyTasksResource

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.comments.resources.CommentManyTasksResource.post
documentation:
Create several comments at once.
---
tags:
- Comments
description: Each comment requires a text, a task id, a task_status and a person as arguments.
             This way, comments keep history of status changes.
             When the comment is created, it updates the task status with given task status.
parameters:
  - in: path
    name: project_id
    required: True
    type: string
    format: UUID
    x-example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
  - in: body
    name: Comment
    description: person ID, name, comment, revision and change status of task
    schema:
        type: object
        required:
            - task_status_id
        properties:
            task_status_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25a4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            comment:
                type: string
            person_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25a4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            object_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25a4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            created_at:
                type: string
                format: date-time
                example: "2022-07-12T13:00:00"
            checklist:
                type: object
                properties:
                    item 1:
                        type: string
responses:
    201:
        description: Given files added to the comment entry as attachments

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.comments.resources.CommentManyTasksResource.get_allowed_comments_only
documentation: def get_allowed_comments_only(self, comments, person):
allowed_comments = []
for comment in comments:
try:
task = tasks_service.get_task_with_relations(
comment["object_id"],
if (
person["role"] == "supervisor"
and (
len(person["departments"]) == 0
or tasks_service.get_task_type(task["task_type_id"])[
"department_id"
in person["departments"]
) or person["id"] in task["assignees"]:
allowed_comments.append(comment)
except permissions.PermissionDenied:
pass
except KeyError:
pass
return allowed_comments
Vrutansh

Create several comments at once. Each comment requires a text, a task id,
a task_status and a person as arguments. This way, comments keep history of
status changes. When the comment is created, it updates the task status with
given task status.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.files.resources.WorkingFilePathResource

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.files.resources.WorkingFilePathResource.post
documentation:
Generate a working file path from file tree template.
---
tags:
- Files
description: "Generate file path based on several parameters: task, software, mode, revision and separator.
             Revision can be computed automatically as next revision if not given."
parameters:
  - in: path
    name: task_id
    required: true
    type: string
    format: UUID
    x-example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
  - in: body
    name: File
    description: Name, software, mode, revision and separator.
    schema:
        type: object
        properties:
            name:
                type: string
                default: main
            mode:
                type: string
                default: working
            software_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            comment:
                type: string
            revision:
                type: integer
            separator:
                type: string
                default: /
responses:
    200:
        description: Working file path generated
    400:
        description: Malformed file tree

Generate from file tree template a working file path based on several
parameters: task, software, mode, revision and separator. Revision can be
computed automatically as next revision if not given.

            extension:

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.files.resources.NewWorkingFileResource.post
documentation:
Create new working file.
---
tags:
- Files
description: A working file is a file used to produce output files.
             It is the file the CG artist is working on.
             It is versioned, tied to a task and a software and requires a comment each time it is created.
             A path is generated for each file created. The path format is defined in the file tree template file.
produces:
  - image/png
  - image/jpg
  - image/gif
  - multipart/form-data
parameters:
  - in: path
    name: task_id
    required: true
    type: string
    format: UUID
    x-example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
  - in: body
    name: File
    description: Name, mode, description, comment, person ID, software ID, revision and separator.
    schema:
        type: object
        required:
          - name
        properties:
            name:
                type: string
            mode:
                type: string
                default: working
            description:
                type: string
            comment:
                type: string
            person_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            software_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            revision:
                type: integer
            sep:
                type: string
                default: /
responses:
    201:
        description: New working file created
        schema:
            type: file
    400:
        description: Given working file already exists

A working file is a file used to produce output files.
It is the file the CG artist is working on.
It is versioned, tied to a task and a software and requires a comment each time it is created.
A path is generated for each file created. The path format is defined in the file tree template file.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.files.resources.NewEntityOutputFileResource

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.files.resources.NewEntityOutputFileResource.post
documentation:
Create new output file linked to a given entity.
---
tags:
- Files
description: Output files are linked to entities.
             Each time a CG artist is satisfied by what he did on a working file,
             he can create an output file that will be linked to a target entity (an asset, a shot, a sequence, ...).
             It keeps track of the working file at the origin of the output file.
             An output type is required for better categorization (textures, caches, ...).
             A task type can be set too to give the department related to the output file.
             Revision is automatically set.
produces:
  - image/png
  - image/jpg
  - image/gif
  - multipart/form-data
parameters:
  - in: path
    name: entity_id
    required: true
    type: string
    format: UUID
    x-example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
  - in: body
    name: File
    description: Name, mode, output type ID, task type ID, person ID, working file ID, file status ID, comment, extension, representation, revision, number of elements and separator.
    schema:
        type: object
        required:
          - output_type_id
          - task_type_id
        properties:
            name:
                type: string
            mode:
                type: string
                default: output
            output_type_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            task_type_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            person_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            working_file_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            file_status_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID
                example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
            comment:
                type: string
            extension:
                type: string
            representation:
                type: string
            revision:
                type: integer
            nb_elements:
                type: integer
                default: 1
            sep:
                type: string
                default: /
responses:
    200:
        description: New output file created
        schema:
            type: file
    400:
        description: Given output file already exists
                     Given person not found
                     Given output type not found

Output files are linked to entities. Each time a CG artist is satisfied
by what he did on a working file, he can create an output file that
will be linked to a target entity (an asset, a shot, a sequence, ...).
It keeps track of the working file at the origin of the output file.
An output type is required for better categorization (textures, caches,
...). A task type can be set too to give the department related to the
output file.

Revision is automatically set.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.files.resources.NewInstanceOutputFileResource

  - in: body
    name: File

    schema:
        type: object
        required:

        properties:

                type: string
                default: main
            mode:
                type: string
                default: output
            output_type_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID

            task_type_id:
                type: string
                format: UUID

            person_id:

                format: UUID

                format: UUID

            revision:

            nb_elements:
                type: integer
                default: 1
            sep:
                type: string
                default: /
responses:

        schema:

    400:

                     Given person not found

Some output files are linked to assets through an instance of this asset
for a give shot. Each time a CG artist is satisfied by what he did on a
working file, he can create an output file that
will be linked to a target instance.
It keeps track of the working file at the origin of the output file.
An output type is required for better categorization (textures, caches,
...). A task type can be set too to give the department related to the
output file.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.previews.resources.UpdateAnnotationsResource.put
documentation:
Allow to modify the annotations stored at the preview level.
---
tags:
  - Previews
description: |
            Modifications are applied via three fields:
            * `annotations` to give all the annotations that need to be added.

            * `updates` that list annotations that needs to be modified.

            * `deletions` to list the IDs of annotations that needs to be removed.
parameters:
  - in: path
    name: preview_file_id
    required: True
    type: string
    format: UUID
    x-example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
responses:
    200:
        description: Orders of previews changed for a single revision

Allow to modify the annotations stored at the preview level.
Modifications are applied via three fields:
* `annotation`s to give all the annotations that need to be added.
* `updates` that list annotations that needs to be modified.
* `deletions` to list the IDs of annotations that needs to be removed.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.auth.resources.LoginResource

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.auth.resources.LoginResource.post
documentation:
Log in user by creating and registering auth tokens.
---
description: Login is based on email and password.
             If no user match given email and a destkop ID, it looks in matching the desktop ID with the one stored in database.
             It is useful for clients that run on desktop tools and that don't know user email.
tags:
    - Authentication
parameters:
  - in: formData
    name: email
    required: True
    type: string
    format: email
    x-example: admin@example.com
  - in: formData
    name: password
    required: True
    type: string
    format: password
    x-example: mysecretpassword
  - in: formData
    name: otp
    required: False
    type: string
    format: password
    x-example: 123456
responses:
  200:
    description: Login successful
  400:
    description: Login failed
  500:
    description: Database not reachable

Log in user by creating and registering auth tokens. Login is based
on email and password. If no user match given email and a destkop ID,
it looks in matching the desktop ID with the one stored in database. It is
useful for clients that run on desktop tools and that don't know user
email.

responses:

classes.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.auth.resources.ChangePasswordResource

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.auth.resources.ChangePasswordResource.post
documentation:
Allow the user to change his password.
---
description: Prior to modifying the password, it requires to give the current password
             (to make sure the user changing the password is not someone who stealed the session).
             The new password requires a confirmation to ensure that the user didn't
             make a mistake by typing his new password.
tags:
    - Authentication
parameters:
  - in: formData
    name: old_password
    required: True
    type: string
    format: password
  - in: formData
    name: password
    required: True
    type: string
    format: password
  - in: formData
    name: password_2
    required: True
    type: string
    format: password
responses:
  200:
    description: Password changed
  400:
    description: Invalid password or inactive user

Allow the user to change his password. Prior to modify the password,
it requires to give the current password (to make sure the user changing
the password is not someone who stealed the session).
The new password requires a confirmation to ensure that the user didn't
make mistake by typing his new password.

classes.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.auth.resources.ResetPasswordResource

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.auth.resources.ResetPasswordResource.put
documentation:
Resource to allow a user to change his password when he forgets it.
---
description: "It uses a classic scheme: a token is sent by email to the user.
             Then he can change his password."
tags:
    - Authentication
parameters:
  - in: formData
    name: email
    required: True
    type: string
    format: email
    x-example: admin@example.com
  - in: formData
    name: token
    required: True
    type: string
    format: JWT token
  - in: formData
    name: password
    required: True
    type: string
    format: password
  - in: formData
    name: password2
    required: True
    type: string
    format: password
responses:
  200:
    description: Password reset
  400:
    description: Invalid password
                 Wrong or expired token
                 Inactive user

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.auth.resources.ResetPasswordResource.post
documentation:
Resource to allow a user to change his password when he forgets it.
---
description: "It uses a classic scheme: a token is sent by email to the user.
             Then he can change his password."

    - Authentication
parameters:
  - in: formData
    name: email
    required: True
    type: string
    format: email
    x-example: admin@example.com
responses:

    description: Reset token sent
  400:
    description: Email not listed in database

Resource to allow a user to change his password when he forgets it.

he can change his password.

cursor_created_at to get the next page.

  - Breakdown
description: It's mainly used for synchronisation purpose.

  - in: path
    name: project_id
    required: True
    type: string
    format: UUID

    name: page
    required: False
    type: string
    format: Number
    x-example: 2
  - in: limit
    name: page
    required: False
    type: string
    format: Number
    x-example: 100
  - in: cursor_created_at
    name: page
    required: False
    type: string
    format: Datetime
    x-example: 2020-01-01T00:00:00
responses:

        description: All entity links related to given project

classes.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.persons.resources.ChangePasswordForPersonResource

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.persons.resources.ChangePasswordForPersonResource.post
documentation:
Allow admin to change password for given user.
---
description: Prior to modifying the password, it requires to be admin.
             An admin can't change other admins password.
             The new password requires a confirmation to ensure that the admin didn't
             make a mistake by typing the new password.
tags:
    - Persons
parameters:
  - in: path
    name: person_id
    required: True
    type: string
    format: UUID
    x-example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
  - in: formData
    name: password
    required: True
    type: string
    format: password
  - in: formData
    name: password_2
    required: True
    type: string
    format: password
responses:
  200:
    description: Password changed
  400:
    description: Invalid password or inactive user
Allow admin to change password for given user.

methods.name: lanstudio.app.blueprints.persons.resources.DisableTwoFactorAuthenticationPersonResource.delete
documentation:
Allow admin to disable two factor authentication for given user.
---
description: Prior to disable two factor authentication, it requires to
             be admin.
             An admin can't disable two factor authentication for other
             admins.
tags:
    - Persons
parameters:
  - in: path
    name: person_id
    required: True
    type: string
    format: UUID
    x-example: a24a6ea4-ce75-4665-a070-57453082c25
responses:
  200:
    description: Two factor authentication disabled
  400:
    description: Inactive user
Allow admin to disable two factor authentication for given user.

This module is named source instead of import because import is a Python

functions.name: lanstudio.app.api.configure
documentation:
Turn Flask app into a REST API. It configures routes, auth and events
system.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.api.configure_api_routes
documentation:
Register blueprints (modules). Each blueprint describe routes and
associated resources (controllers).

functions.name: lanstudio.app.api.register_event_handlers
documentation:
Load code from event handlers folder. Then it registers in the event manager
each event handler listed in the __init_.py.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.api.load_plugins
documentation: Load plugin, (bunch of resources dedicated to a specific usage).

functions.name: lanstudio.app.api.load_plugin_modules
documentation:
Run Python import on all plugin listed in plugin folder. It returns the
imported module.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.api.load_plugin
documentation:
Load a given plugin as an API plugin: add configured routes to the API. It
assumes that the plugin is already loaded in memory has a blueprint
structure.

It's useful to generate callbacks for event listener.

Shot metadata changes are versioned. This function returns all versions

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.shots_service.get_weighted_quotas
documentation:
Build quota statistics. It counts the number of frames done for each day.
A shot is considered done at the first feedback request. If time spent is
filled for it, it weights the result with the frame number with the time
spents. If there is no time spent, it considers that the work was done
from the wip date to the feedback date.
It computes the shot count and the number of seconds too.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.shots_service.get_raw_quotas
documentation:
Build quota statistics in a raw way. It counts the number of frames done
for each day. A shot is considered done at the first feedback request (end
date). It considers that all the work was done at the end date.
It computes the shot count and the number of seconds too.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.shots_service.get_month_quota_shots
documentation:
Return shots that are included in quota comptutation for given
person and month.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.shots_service.get_week_quota_shots
documentation:

person and week.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.shots_service.get_day_quota_shots
documentation:
Return shots that are included in quota comptutation for given
person and day.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.shots_service.get_weighted_quota_shots_between
documentation:
Get all shots leading to a quota computation during the given period.
Set a weight on each one:
    * If there is time spent filled, weight it by the sum of duration
      divided py the overall task duration.
    * If there is no time spent, weight it by the number of business days
      in the time interval spent between WIP date (start) and
      feedback date (end).

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.files_service.create_new_output_revision
documentation:
Create a new ouput file for given entity. Output type, task type, author
and source file are required.

The revision is set as next revision available but it can be forced.
An extension and a name can be set too.

An asset instance can be given too. In that case, the output file is
linked to the asset instance.

The `temporal_entity_id` concerns only asset instance output files. It is
here to describe if the output is generated in the context of a shot or in
the context of a scene.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.create_person
documentation:
Create a new person entry in the database. No operation are performed on
password, so encrypted password is expected.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.update_password
documentation: Update password field for use matching given email.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.update_person
documentation: Update person entry with data given in parameter.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.delete_person
documentation: Delete person entry from database.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.get_desktop_login_logs
documentation: Get all logs for user desktop logins.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.create_desktop_login_logs
documentation: Add a new log entry for desktop logins.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.update_person_last_presence
documentation:
Update person presence field with the most recent time spent or
desktop login log.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.get_presence_logs
documentation:
Return arrays of presence for a given month, adapted for a CSV rendering.
Rows are users and columns represent the days of given month.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.invite_person
documentation:
Send an invitation email to given person (a mail telling him/her how to
connect on Kitsu).

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.get_organisation
documentation: Return organisation set up on this instance. It creates it if none exists.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.update_organisation
documentation: Update organisation entry with data given in parameter.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.is_user_limit_reached
documentation:
Returns true if the number of active users is equal and superior to the
user limit set in the configuration.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.add_to_department
documentation: Add to department.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.remove_from_department
documentation: Remove from department.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.persons_service.clear_avatar
documentation: Set person `has_avatar` field to False and delete related file.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.base_service.get_instance
documentation:
Get instance of any model from its ID and raise given exception if not
found.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.base_service.get_or_create_instance_by_name
documentation:
Get instance of any model by name. If it doesn't exist it creates a new
instance of this model from positional arguments dict.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.base_service.get_model_map_from_array
documentation:
Return a map matching based on given model list. The maps keys are the model
IDs and the values are the models. It's convenient to check find a model by
its ID.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.check_auth
documentation:
Depending on configured strategy, it checks if given email and password
mach an active user in the database. It raises exceptions adapted to
encountered error (no auth strategy configured, wrong email, wrong passwor
or unactive user).
App is needed as parameter to give access to configuration while avoiding
cyclic imports.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.no_password_auth_strategy
documentation: No password auth strategy

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.local_auth_strategy
documentation:
Local strategy just checks that person and passwords are correct the
traditional way (email is in database and related password hash corresponds
to given password).
Password hash comparison is based on BCrypt.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.ldap_auth_strategy
documentation:
Connect to an active directory server to know if given user can be
authenticated.
When person is not from ldap, it can try to connect with default auth
strategy.
(only if fallback is activated (via LDAP_FALLBACK flag) in configuration)

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.check_login_failed_attemps
documentation: Checks that the person has not reached the failed login limit.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.update_login_failed_attemps
documentation: Update login failed attemps for a person_id.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.check_two_factor_authentication
documentation: Check multifactor authentication for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.remove_otp_revovery_code
documentation: Remove an otp recovery code for a person_id.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.check_totp
documentation: Check TOTP for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.check_email_otp
documentation: Check email OTP for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.check_recovery_code
documentation: Check recovery code for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.check_fido
documentation: Check fido for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.pre_enable_totp
documentation: Pre-enable TOTP for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.enable_totp
documentation: Enable TOTP for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.disable_totp
documentation: Disable TOTP for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.pre_enable_email_otp
documentation: Pre-enable mail OTP for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.enable_email_otp
documentation: Enable mail OTP for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.disable_email_otp
documentation: Disable email OTP for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.send_email_otp
documentation: Send an email with OTP and store the OTP for checking after.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.pre_register_fido
documentation: Pre-register FIDO device for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.register_fido
documentation: Register FIDO device for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.unregister_fido
documentation: Unregister FIDO device for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.get_challenge_fido
documentation: Get new FIDO challenge for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.generate_new_recovery_codes
documentation: Generate new recovery codes for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.register_tokens
documentation:
Register access and refresh tokens to auth token store. That way they
can be used like a session.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.revoke_tokens
documentation: Remove access and refresh tokens from auth token store.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.is_default_password
documentation: Check if password is default.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.generate_reset_token
documentation: Generate and return a reset token.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.generate_recovery_codes
documentation: Generate and return recovery codes.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.hash_recovery_codes
documentation: Hash recovery codes given as argument and return them.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.auth_service.check_login_failed_attemps
documentation: Check login failed attemps for a person.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_casting
documentation:
Return all assets and their number of occurences listed in given shot
(or asset for set dressing).

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_production_episodes_casting
documentation:
Return all assets and their number of occurences listed in episodes of given
project. Result is returned as a map where keys are asset IDs and values
are casting for given episode.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_sequence_casting
documentation:
Return all assets and their number of occurences listed in shots of given
sequence. Result is returned as a map where keys are shot IDs and values
are casting for given shot.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_all_sequences_casting
documentation:
Return all assets and their number of occurences listed in shots of given
project and episode.  Result is returned as a map where keys are shot IDs
and values are casting for given shot.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_asset_type_casting
documentation:
Return all assets and their number of occurences listed in asset of given
asset type. Result is returned as a map where keys are asset IDs and values
are casting for given asset.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.update_casting
documentation:
Update casting for given entity. Casting is an array of dictionaries made of
two fields: `asset_id` and `nb_occurences`.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.create_casting_link
documentation: Add a link between given entity and given asset.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service._create_episode_casting_link
documentation:
When an asset is casted in a shot, the asset is automatically casted in
the episode.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_cast_in
documentation: Get the list of shots where an asset is casted in.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_asset_instances_for_scene
documentation:
Return all asset instances for given scene.

Asset instances are a different way to represent the casting of a shot.
They allow to track precisely output files generated when building a shot.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_asset_instances_for_shot
documentation: Return asset instances casted in given shot.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_shot_asset_instances_for_asset
documentation: Return asset instances casted in a shot for given asset.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_scene_asset_instances_for_asset
documentation: Return all asset instances of an asset casted in layout scenes.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_camera_instances_for_scene
documentation: Return all instances of type Camera for given layout scene.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.add_asset_instance_to_scene
documentation: Create a new asset instance for given asset and scene.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.add_asset_instance_to_shot
documentation: Add asset instance to instance casting of given shot.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.remove_asset_instance_for_shot
documentation: Remove asset instance from instance casting of given shot.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.build_asset_instance_name
documentation:
Helpers to generate normalized asset instance name. It is used to build
default instance names.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_asset_instances_for_asset
documentation:
Return all asset instances inside given asset.

Asset instances for asset are used for environment (instantiation of props)
or for props (instantiation of sub props).

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.add_asset_instance_to_asset
documentation: Create a new asset instance for given asset and scene.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_entity_casting
documentation: Get entities related to entity as external entities.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_entity_link_raw
documentation: Get link matching given entities.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.get_entity_link
documentation: Get link matching given entities.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.refresh_casting_stats
documentation:
For each shot including given asset, for all related tasks, it computes
how many assets are available for this task and saves the result
on the task level.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.breakdown_service.refresh_shot_casting_stats
documentation:
For all tasks related to given shot, it computes how many assets are
available for this task and saves the result on the task level.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.events.register
documentation:
Register a listener by linking an event name to an handler module.
The handler module exposes a function named `handle_event` which is executed
when linked event occurs.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.events.register_all
documentation:
Register all listeners described by the event map. The key is the event
name, the value is the module that must be loaded when event occurs

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.events.unregister
documentation:
Remove handler with given name from registered listener list. The handler
will be no more executed when the event occurs.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.events.unregister_all
documentation:
All event handlers are removed. Nothing will be executed anymore when an
event occurs.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.events.emit
documentation:
Emit an event which leads to the execution of all event handlers registered
for that event name.
It publishes too the event to other services
(like the realtime event daemon).

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.events.save_event
documentation: Store event information in the database.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.emails.send_email
documentation: Send an email with given subject and body to given recipient.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.string.strtobool
documentation:
Convert a string (val) to a boolean value.
If val is already a boolean return val.
Else raise ValueError.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.fields.serialize_value
documentation:
Utility function to handle the normalizing of specific fields.
The aim is to make the result JSON serializable

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.fields.serialize_list
documentation:
Serialize a list of any kind of objects into data structures
that are JSON serializable.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.fields.serialize_dict
documentation:
Serialize a dict of any kind of objects into data structures that are JSON
serializable.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.fields.serialize_orm_arrays
documentation: Serialize a orm array into simple data structures (useful for json dumping).

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.fields.serialize_models
documentation: Serialize a list of models (useful for json dumping)

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.fields.gen_uuid
documentation: Generate a unique identifier (useful for json dumping).

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.fields.get_date_object
documentation:
Shortcut for date parsing (useful for json dumping).
Format for full date string: %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.fields.get_default_date_object
documentation: Shortcut for date parsing at default format.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.env.envtobool
documentation:
Convert an environment variable to a boolean value.
If environment variable can't be converted raise ValueError.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.env.env_with_semicolon_to_list
documentation:
Convert an environment variable to a list.
Items are separated by semicolon.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.dbhelpers.reset_all
documentation: Check that database exist.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.dbhelpers.create_all
documentation: Create all database tables.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.dbhelpers.drop_all
documentation: Drop all database tables.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.build_assignee_filter
documentation: Query filter for task to retrieve only tasks assigned to current user.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.build_team_filter
documentation:
Query filter for task to retrieve only models from project for which the
user is part of the team.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.build_open_project_filter
documentation: Query filter for project to retrieve only open projects.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.build_related_projects_filter
documentation:
Query filter for project to retrieve open projects of which the user
is part of the team.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.related_projects
documentation:
Return all projects related to current user: open projects of which the user
is part of the team.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_todos
documentation: Get all unfinished tasks assigned to current user.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_done_tasks
documentation: Get all finished tasks assigned to current user for open projects.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_tasks_to_check
documentation: Get all tasks waiting for feedback in the user department.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_tasks_for_entity
documentation: Get all tasks assigned to current user and related to given entity.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_task_types_for_entity
documentation:
Get all task types of tasks assigned to current user and related to given
entity.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_assets_for_asset_type
documentation:
Get all assets for given asset type anp project and for which user has
a task related.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_asset_types_for_project
documentation:
Get all asset types for which there is an asset for which current user has a
task assigned. Assets are listed in given project.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_sequences_for_project
documentation:
Return all sequences for given project and for which current user has
a task assigned to a shot.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_project_episodes
documentation:
Return all episodes for given project and for which current user has
a task assigned to a shot.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_shots_for_sequence
documentation: Get all shots for given sequence and for which the user has a task assigned.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_scenes_for_sequence
documentation:
Get all layout scenes for given sequence and for which the user has a task
assigned.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_open_projects
documentation: Get all open projects for which current user is part of the team.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_open_project_ids
documentation: Get all open project ids for which current user is part of the team.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_projects
documentation: Get all projects for which current user has a task assigned.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_working_on_entity
documentation: Return True if user has task assigned which is related to given entity.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_working_on_task
documentation: Return True if user has task assigned.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_person_access
documentation: Return True if user is an admin or is matching given person id.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_belong_to_project
documentation:
Return true if current user is assigned to a task of the given project or
if current_user is part of the project team.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_project_access
documentation:
Return true if current user is a manager or has a task assigned for this
project.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.block_access_to_vendor
documentation: Raise PermissionDenied if current user has a vendor role.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_entity_access
documentation:
Return true if current user is not a vendor or has a task assigned for this
project.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_comment_access
documentation: Return true if current user can have access to a comment.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_manager_project_access
documentation:
Return true if current user is a manager and has a task assigned for this
project.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_supervisor_project_access
documentation:
Return true if current user is a manager or a supervisor and has a task
assigned for this project.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_supervisor_task_access
documentation:
Return true if current user is a manager and has a task assigned related
to the project of this task or is a supervisor and can modify data accorded
to his departments

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_supervisor_schedule_item_access
documentation:
Return true if current user is a manager and has a task assigned related
to the project of this task or is a supervisor and can modify data accorded
to his departments

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_metadata_department_access
documentation:
Return true if current user is a manager and has a task assigned for this
project or is a supervisor and is allowed to modify data accorded to
his departments

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_task_departement_access
documentation:
Return true if current user is an admin or is a manager and is in team
or is a supervisor in the department of the task or is an artist assigning
himself in the department of the task.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_task_departement_access_for_unassign
documentation:
Return true if current user is an admin or is a manager and is in team
or is a supervisor in the department of the task or is an artist assigning
himself in the department of the task.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_all_departments_access
documentation:
Return true if current user is admin or is manager and is in team or is
supervisor and is in team and have access to all departments.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.check_day_off_access
documentation: Return true if current user is admin or day_off is for itself

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_filters
documentation:
Retrieve search filters used by current user. It groups them by
list type and project_id. If the filter is not related to a project,
the project_id is all.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_user_filters
documentation:
Retrieve search filters used for given user. It groups them by
list type and project_id. If the filter is not related to a project,
the project_id is all.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.create_filter
documentation: Add a new search filter to the database.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.update_filter
documentation: Update given filter from database.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.remove_filter
documentation: Remove given filter from database.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_filter_groups
documentation:
Retrieve search filter groups used by current user. It groups them by
list type and project_id. If the filter group is not related to a project,
the project_id is all.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_user_filter_groups
documentation:
Retrieve search filter groups used for given user. It groups them by
list type and project_id. If the filter group is not related to a project,
the project_id is all.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.create_filter_group
documentation: Add a new search filter group to the database.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_filter_group
documentation: Get given filter group from the database.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.update_filter_group
documentation: Update given filter group from database.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.remove_filter_group
documentation: Remove given filter group from database.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_notification
documentation: Return notification matching given ID as a dictionnary.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_unread_notifications_count
documentation: Return the number of unread notifications.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_last_notifications
documentation: Return last 100 user notifications.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.mark_notifications_as_read
documentation:
Mark all recent notifications for current_user as read. It is useful
to mark a list of notifications as read after an user retrieved them.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.has_task_subscription
documentation:
Returns true if a subscription entry exists for current user and given
task.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.subscribe_to_task
documentation: Create a subscription entry for current user and given task

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.unsubscribe_from_task
documentation: Remove subscription entry for current user and given task

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.has_sequence_subscription
documentation:
Returns true if a subscription entry exists for current user and given
sequence.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.subscribe_to_sequence
documentation: Create a subscription entry for current user and given sequence

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.unsubscribe_from_sequence
documentation: Remove subscription entry for current user and given sequence

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.user_service.get_sequence_subscriptions
documentation:
Return list of sequence ids for which the current user has subscriptions
for given project and task type.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.file_tree_service.get_working_file_path
documentation:
Return working file path based on given paramaters. The task is mandatory
to get the whole context. The mode matches a template described in the
file tree file. Software, output type and name are required only if they
are set in the template.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.file_tree_service.get_data_from_token
documentation: Get the first corresponding data using the given type and value tokens.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.services.file_tree_service.guess_from_path
lanstudio.app.utils.csv_utils.build_csv_response
documentation: Construct a Flask response that returns content of a csv a file.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.csv_utils.build_csv_file_name
documentation: Add application name as prefix of the file name.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.csv_utils.build_csv_string
documentation: Build a CSV formatted string from an array.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.csv_utils.build_csv_headers
documentation: Build HTTP response headers needed to return CSV content as a file.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.permissions.has_manager_permissions
documentation: Return True if user is an admin or a manager.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.permissions.has_admin_permissions
documentation: Return True if user is an admin.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.permissions.has_client_permissions
documentation: Return True if user is a client.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.permissions.has_vendor_permissions
documentation: Return True if user is a vendor.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.permissions.has_supervisor_permissions
documentation: Return True if user is a supervisor.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.permissions.has_at_least_supervisor_permissions
documentation: Return True if user is an admin or a manager.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.permissions.check_at_least_supervisor_permissions
documentation:
Return True if user is admin, manager or supervsior. It raises a
PermissionDenied exception in case of failure.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.permissions.check_manager_permissions
documentation:
Return True if user is admin or manager. It raises a PermissionDenied
exception in case of failure.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.permissions.check_admin_permissions
documentation:
Return True if user is admin. It raises a PermissionDenied exception in case
of failure.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.query.get_query_criterions_from_request
documentation:
Turn request parameters into a dict where keys are attributes to filter and
values are values to filter.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.query.apply_criterions_to_db_query
documentation: Apply criterions given in HTTP request to the sqlachemy db query object.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.query.get_paginated_results
documentation: Apply pagination to the query object.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.query.apply_sort_by
documentation: Apply an order by clause to a sqlalchemy query from a string parameter.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.flask.output_json
documentation: Makes a Flask response with a JSON encoded body

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.api.configure_api_from_blueprint
documentation:
Creates a Flask Restful api object based on information from given
blueprint. API is configured to return JSON objects.

Each blueprint is describe by a list of tuple. Each tuple is composed of a
route and the related resource (controller).

modules.name: lanstudio.app.utils.cache
documentation:
This module is a wrapper for flask_caching. It configures it and rename
the memoize function. The aim with that cache is to minimize the requests
made on the target database.

documentation: coding: utf-8

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.commands.clean_auth_tokens
documentation:
Remove all revoked tokens (most of the time outdated) from the key value
store.

documentation: Remove all authentication tokens from the key value store.

documentation: Put the minimum required data into the database to start with it.

documentation: Connect to a LDAP server, then creates all related accounts.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.commands.import_data_from_another_instance
documentation:
Retrieve and save all the data from another API instance. It doesn't
change the IDs.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.commands.run_sync_change_daemon
documentation:
Listen to event websocket. Each time a change occurs, it retrieves the
related data and save it in the current instance.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.commands.run_sync_file_change_daemon
documentation:
Listen to event websocket. Each time a change occurs, it retrieves the
related file and save it in the current instance storage.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.commands.import_last_changes_from_another_instance
documentation:
Retrieve and save all the data related to most recent events from another
API instance. It doesn't change the IDs.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.commands.import_last_file_changes_from_another_instance
documentation:
Retrieve and save all the data related most to recent file events
from another API instance (new previews and thumbnails).
It doesn't change the IDs.

functions.name: lanstudio.app.utils.commands.import_files_from_another_instance
documentation:
Retrieve and save all the data related most recent events from another API
instance. It doesn't change the IDs.
